Off Calendar Apr-28-2015 Continued To Oct-26-15 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Notice Of Motion And Motion For An Order Reopening Discovery And Compelling Attendance At Deposition And For Sanctions
Off Calendar May-28-2015 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Sharon R. Vinick, of Levy Vinick Burrell Hyams, Tel. 510.318.7702, for plaintiffs;
Rony Sagy, of Sagy Law Associates LLP, Tel. 415.986.0900, for defendants;
Barbara L. Gately, of Sagy Law Associates LLP, Tel. 415.986.0900, for defendants.

Ruling: A R G U E D. The Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANT NEW DEAL DESIGN LLC'S Motion For An Order Reopening Discovery And Compelling Attendance At Deposition And For Sanctions is Denied. CCP sec. 2025.610 provides that a natural person, including a party to the action, can only be deposed once. If a party wants to take a subsequent deposition of a natural person, it can do so only after obtaining leave of court for good cause shown or if the parties stipulate that a subsequent deposition may be taken. (CCP sec. 20225.610(b).) Defendant already deposed Plaintiff for three days. Defendant failed to comply with CCP sec. 2025.610 and therefore, the motion to compel an improperly noticed deposition is denied. The notice was also defective because Plaintiff resided out of state at the time the notice was served. (CCP secs. 1989 and 2025.250(a).) The motion for an order reopening discovery is denied. The evidence establishes that Defendant did not act diligently in pursuing additionally discovery. Pursuant to CCP sec. 2024.050(c), Defendant shall pay Plaintiff sanctions in the amount of $4,085 within 30 days of entry of this order. Order signed in open court. Judge Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk Felicia Green; Court Reporter Melanie Gheno, CSR No. 7489; Dept. 302. (302/EHG)

Off Calendar Dec-16-2014 Denied
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES: Atty. SHARON R. VINICK and JEAN HYAMS of Levy Vinick Burrell Hyams, LLP, Tel No. (510) 318-7702 appearing for the Plaintiff;
Atty. RONY SAGY and BARBARA GATELY of Sagy Law Associates, Tel No. (415) 986-0900 appearing for the Defendant.

RULING - The matter was not argued. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF LAUREN HAULE'S Motion To Strike Portions Of Defendant's Statement of Undisputed Material Facts In Support Of Motion For Summary Judgment Or, in the Alternative, Summary Adjudication of Issues is DENIED. The Court considered only the material facts contained in Defendant's Statement and disregarded any opinion, argument or other improper matter presented therein. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Sheryl L. Sawyer, CSR #5976 (302/EHG)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, For Summary Adjudication Of Issues
Off Calendar Dec-16-2014 Denied
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES: Atty. SHARON R. VINICK and JEAN HYAMS of Levy Vinick Burrell Hyams, LLP, Tel No. (510) 318-7702 appearing for the Plaintiff;
Atty. RONY SAGY and BARBARA GATELY of Sagy Law Associates, Tel No. (415) 986-0900 appearing for the Defendant.

RULING - A R G U E D - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT NEW DEAL DESIGN LLC'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, For Summary Adjudication Of Issues is DENIED. First, Plaintiff has presented sufficient evidence to establish a prima facie case of sex discrimination. Defendant's assertion that Plaintiff was not a member of the protected class is unavailing, as the statute only requires that the discrimination be because of pregnancy, not during pregnancy. (See Gov. Code 12940(a).) Additionally, triable issues of material exist as to whether Plaintiff was competently performing at the time of termination (Plaintiff's UMF #22, 71, 107) and whether Defendant exhibited discriminatory animus toward Plaintiff as a result of her pregnancy (Plaintiff's UMF #35, 37 - 39). Defendant's hearsay objection is denied. (See Evid. Code 1220-27.) Plaintiff has also met her burden of establishing the existence of triable issues of material fact as to whether Defendant had a legitimate business reason for Plaintiff's termination. (See Defendant's UMF #155, 156; Plaintiff's UMF #80, 82-85, 87, 89-97.) Second, Defendant is not entitled to judgment as a matter of law for either remaining cause of action. Defendant presents evidence establishing only that CFRA and FMLA do not apply. However, Defendant's second cause of action is based on the public policy delineated in the FEHA, which the Motion fails to address. Finally, Defendant presents no evidence that Plaintiff failed to exhaust her administrative remedies with respect to Plaintiff's third cause of action, and there is a triable issue of material fact as to whether the parties agreed upon a date of reinstatement. (See 2 CCR 11043(b)(1); Plaintiff's UMF # 58, 60-62.) ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Court returned Defendant's conditionally-lodged-under-seal Exhibit Binder in Support of Motion for Summary Judgment (11 envelopes). Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Sheryl L. Sawyer, CSR #5976 (302/EHG)

Notice Of Motion And Moiton To Compel Production Of Documents Pursuant To Pltf'S Request For Production Of Documents, Set Two
Off Calendar Apr-29-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance.

RULING: The Court adopted the tentative ruling which was not contested. PLAINTIFF LAUREN HAULE'S MOTION TO COMPEL PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS PURSUANT TO PLAINTIFF'S REQUEST FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS, SET TWO is Granted, as to Request nos. 53 - 58, 63 and Denied as to Request nos. 59 - 62. Defendant has placed Plaintiff's non-work related emails at issue in this case. Plaintiff is entitled to conduct limited discovery into whether certain other employees engaged in similar conduct. Within 20 days, Defendant New Deal Design, LLC ("Defendant") shall serve further responses and produce responsive documents. For Request nos. 53 - 58, 63, except for President and founder Gadi Ami, the non-work related activities of other employees is discoverable. The discovery sought is limited to emails sent by employees with certain phrases or links and does not implicate the privacy rights of the third party employees. Defendant has not demonstrated that this discovery is unduly burdensome. The emails that are the subject of the discovery requests are limited to specific subject lines. For Request nos. 59 - 60, Defendant has responded to these requests, and they are no longer at issue. For Request no. 61 - 62, as the President and founder, Mr. Ami is not a similarly situated comparator to Plaintiff. The probative value is insufficient to warrant discovery. Monetary sanctions are denied. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Reporter: Giselle Casey CSR# 8098.
